title: centaurea horrida
intro: Centaurea horrida, also known as rough knapweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Centaurea horrida have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, can be made from the plant's leaves and flowers to harness its medicinal properties.
